Class GameManager

java.lang.Object
core.GameManager
public class GameManager extends Object
Global tracker for the current state of the game.

GameManager is a static utility class — all fields and methods are static because there is only ever one game running at a time. It is responsible for:

  • Tracking whose turn it is and advancing turns
  • Detecting the setup phase (negative turn count)
  • Querying the board for units by tile position
  • Maintaining the currently selected unit

Turn numbering starts at -players.length so that each player gets one "setup turn" before normal gameplay begins. Once the count reaches 0 the game is live.

    Returns true if an allied unit (a unit belonging to the same player as unit) occupies the given tile.

    Returns true if an enemy unit (a unit belonging to a different player than unit) occupies the given tile.

  • Field Details

    • players

      public static Player[] players
      The two players in the current game. Index 0 = Player 1, index 1 = Player 2.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameManager

      public GameManager()

  • Method Details

    • setSelectedUnit

      public static void setSelectedUnit(Unit unit)
      Sets the unit that the active player has selected.
      Parameters:
      unit - The unit to select.

    • getSelectedUnit

      public static Unit getSelectedUnit()
      Returns the currently selected unit, or null if nothing is selected.
      Returns:
      The selected unit, or null.

    • clearSelection

      public static void clearSelection()
      Clears the current unit selection.

    • getUnitAtTile

      public static Unit getUnitAtTile(int row, int col)
      Returns the unit occupying a given tile, or null if the tile is empty.
      Parameters:
      row - The row of the tile to check.
      col - The column of the tile to check.
      Returns:
      The unit at (row, col), or null if unoccupied.

    • isTileBlocked

      public static boolean isTileBlocked(int row, int col)
      Returns true if any unit (ally or enemy) occupies the given tile.
      Parameters:
      row - The row to check.
      col - The column to check.
      Returns:
      true if the tile is occupied.

    • isEnemyAtTile

      public static boolean isEnemyAtTile(int row, int col, Unit unit)
      Returns true if an enemy unit (a unit belonging to a different player than unit) occupies the given tile.
      Parameters:
      row - The row to check.
      col - The column to check.
      unit - The reference unit used to determine ownership.
      Returns:
      true if the tile holds an enemy of unit.

    • isAllyAtTile

      public static boolean isAllyAtTile(int row, int col, Unit unit)
      Returns true if an allied unit (a unit belonging to the same player as unit) occupies the given tile.
      Parameters:
      row - The row to check.
      col - The column to check.
      unit - The reference unit used to determine ownership.
      Returns:
      true if the tile holds an ally of unit.

    • getActivePlayerID

      public static int getActivePlayerID()
      Returns the ID of the player whose turn it currently is.
      Returns:
      The active player ID (0 or 1), or -1 before the game starts.

    • startGame

      public static void startGame()
      Initialises turn tracking and determines the first active player.

      The turn count is set to -players.length so that each player gets one setup turn (for unit placement) before the regular game begins. Call this once after both players have been created.

    • endTurn

      public static void endTurn()
      Ends the active player's turn, resets their units' movement, and advances to the next player. Also clears any current unit selection.

      Safe to call before the game has started — it checks bounds before touching the players array.

    • getTurnCount

      public static int getTurnCount()
      Returns the current turn count. Negative values indicate the setup phase; zero and above indicate normal gameplay.
      Returns:
      The current turn count.

    • isSetupTurn

      public static boolean isSetupTurn()
      Returns true if the game is still in the setup phase, where players are placing their initial units. The setup phase lasts while the turn count is negative (one setup turn per player).
      Returns:
      true during the setup phase, false once gameplay begins.

    • getPlayerOfUnit

      public static int getPlayerOfUnit(Unit unit)
      Returns the player ID for a given unit as an int.
      Parameters:
      unit - The unit to query.
      Returns:
      The player ID that owns the unit (0 or 1).

    • getActivePlayer

      public static Player getActivePlayer()
      Returns the Player object for the player whose turn it currently is.
      Returns:
      The active player.

    • getPositionOfUnit

      public static int[] getPositionOfUnit(Unit unit)
      Searches every tile on the board to find where the given unit is located.
      Parameters:
      unit - The unit to locate.
      Returns:
      An int[] of {row, col}, or {-1, -1} if the unit is not found on the board.
